Afro+ Fest Deserves.

The Origin
This Started In a
Vendor Tent. Year One.
At the very first Afro+ Fest, I worked alongside my cousin at her skincare booth. It was a great product and we were in a prime, high-traffic location, but we still watched the money walk right past us all weekend.
We spent that day waving people down and calling out, fighting to earn three seconds of attention from a crowd that never stopped moving. No matter how hard we worked the booth, the result was the same.
The product was never the problem, because the real problem was information. Thousands of people walked past that table, and not one of them knew what was on it. You simply cannot buy what you never knew existed.
I have owned businesses and also thrown events where there were vendors. Standing on both sides, with that perspective, is what allowed me to see the opportunity that both sides are leaving on the table. So I built the solution.
The Problem
A Festival Leaking
Revenue and Loyalty.
That one booth was a symptom of a structural problem across the entire event. When people lack information they do not engage, and when they do not engage, the festival loses revenue on every side. Four costly gaps define the problem.
🚶🏾
Attendees Walk Past Vendors
Foot traffic is not the same as sales, because people will not commit to a booth they know nothing about. The vendor never gets a fair chance at the sale.
📍
Attendees Experience a Fraction of the Event
Many attendees claim a single spot and hold it all night, experiencing only a sliver of the festival they paid for. When the day does not live up to the ticket price, they think twice about buying again.
📦
Vendors Operate Without Data
Vendors have no visibility into what is selling or when to restock. A vendor who sells out early or brings the wrong inventory is a vendor who does not return the following year.
📊
The Festival Captures No Data
When the weekend ends, every bit of intelligence disappears with it. Without vendor metrics or attendee patterns, the festival is forced to rebuild from nothing each year.
The Opportunity · The Attendee
Consider
Sarah.
Sarah is flying in from Atlanta for her first time in DC and her first time at Northwest Stadium. She is excited for the weekend, but she is also navigating an unfamiliar city and a massive venue without a map.
When she opens the festival platform on her phone, every vendor, the main stage, and every food option is laid out in front of her. She browses a skincare booth and builds a wishlist before she has even reached the gate.
Sarah arrives with a plan, knowing where to go, what she wants to buy, and when to buy it before it sells out. She spends the weekend confident, spending, and experiencing the festival in full.
That is the difference between an attendee who sees one corner of the event and an attendee who experiences all of it, and the second one is the one who buys a ticket again next year.
The Opportunity · The Vendor
Consider
Marcus.
Marcus sells body care from Booth A-12, and on day one he runs his booth the way nearly every vendor does. He operates on instinct, hopes for traffic, and has no real way of knowing whether he brought the right inventory.
This year, Marcus has a dashboard, and a single tap logs every sale. By midday, the platform shows him that his body scrub is moving fast with only seven units left, and it hands him a ready-to-post promotion to sell the rest.
By the end of the night, he knows exactly what sold and restocks for day two with precision. There are no sellouts, no dead inventory, and no guesswork, only a stronger and more profitable second day.
Vendors who turn a profit come back, and vendors who come back fill the festival and refer others. That cycle is the engine behind a festival that grows stronger year over year.
The Opportunity · The Vendor Coordinator
Consider the
Vendor Coordinator.
The vendor coordinator and their team are the people working with vendors every single day. They handle setup, placement, power, payments, and every question in between, and on event day they are the first call for more than a hundred vendors at once.
The problem is volume. The same routine questions come in over and over, all day long, and one team cannot be in a hundred places at the same time. Vendors wait, frustration builds, and the coordinator spends the festival putting out fires instead of actually running vendor operations.
This platform gives every vendor a dashboard that answers the routine questions on its own, with a direct line for anything that genuinely needs a person. The repetitive load disappears, and the coordinator's team is freed up for the work that actually requires them.
The platform does not replace the vendor coordinator. It removes the busywork that buries them, so the team that knows the vendors best can focus on the vendors who need them most.
The Opportunity · The Founder
Consider
Mike.
Mike built Afro+ Fest from the ground up, and on event day he is responsible for every moving part of it. He is the founder and CEO, and the entire festival runs on the decisions he makes in the moment.
The problem is that he makes those decisions on partial information. He needs to know how vendors are performing, where the crowd is concentrated, and what is working before it becomes a problem. Today, that picture reaches him late, secondhand, and incomplete.
This platform puts the entire festival on Mike's phone. The Command Center shows him crowd activity, vendor performance, and emerging issues the moment they appear, so he can act on what is actually happening instead of what he is told an hour later.
After the festival, Mike holds something he has never had before. Hard data on his own event. Real numbers to take to sponsors, to set vendor pricing, and to build a bigger, more profitable festival next year.
The Solution
One Platform.
Four Experiences.
The Afro+ Fest AI Suite is a fully managed platform that runs the festival's technology from one place. Attendees find what they came for. Vendors sell on real data. Mike sees the whole event in real time. The Elevate AI team operates the system from start to finish.

✨ Where the AI Drives Real Value
For attendees: the AI learns each person's individual preferences and surfaces what they are most likely to buy, so relevant recommendations translate directly into higher spend.
For Mike: the AI analyzes what thousands of attendees search for and turns that live demand into a clear vendor roadmap for next year.
For vendors: the AI reports exactly what sold and how quickly, so every vendor restocks on evidence rather than instinct. Profitable vendors are what compound this festival's growth.
Platform Deep Dive
Four Builds. One
Connected System.
Build 01
Attendee Discovery App
Up to 60,000 People Each Day · No Download Required
  • GPS-powered live vendor map. Locate any booth instantly.
  • Full vendor catalogs, browsable from anywhere on the grounds
  • Wishlist, route planning, and budgeting in one place
  • Live low-stock alerts. "Only 3 left at Booth A-12."
  • Zuri AI concierge. Instant answers to any festival question.
  • Push alerts for schedule changes and announcements
Build 02
Vendor AI Dashboard
100+ Vendors · Built for One-Tap Use
  • One-tap "Sold One" tracking. No spreadsheets, no friction.
  • Revenue Pulse Score. A live performance read all day.
  • Zuri sales coaching. Targeted prompts to lift each vendor's revenue.
  • Inventory velocity alerts. "Selling fast. 7 left." With a ready promotion.
  • Lead capture for post-festival follow-up and repeat sales
  • Post-festival performance report, delivered within 7 days
Build 03
Owner Command Center
Mike & the Afro+ Team
  • Live zone activity. See where the crowd is moving.
  • Vendor performance across all 100+ booths
  • Zuri as Chief of Staff. Morning briefings, daily summaries.
  • Role-based access. Leadership sees all, the team sees ops.
  • Year-over-year dashboard. Year 3 comparison built in.
Build 04
Elevate Ops Center
Elevate AI Growth Solutions Team Only
  • Live support ticket queue with urgency triage
  • System health monitor. Real-time platform status.
  • Cost tracking in real time
  • Vendor oversight. Flag and resolve issues fast.
  • The Elevate AI team runs this all 3 days. Leadership never touches it.
Proof of Work
All Four Builds Are
Designed and Built.

Why Elevate AI
Built by Someone Who
Lived the Problem.
"I am not pitching this as an outsider who has never been to the event. I was at the first festival, and I felt for the vendors who were not making the sales they came for. I also understand that the festival itself is the main attraction, and that is the name of the game. What I built is a way for both sides to make more money, without either one competing for attention."
— Zuleema Isaac, Director of Festival AI Systems · Elevate AI Growth Solutions
🏗️
Engineered for Festival Scale
The platform is built to support 60,000 people on-site every day across all 3 days. Enterprise-grade infrastructure, with zero technical burden on your team.

A Dedicated Team On-Site
The Elevate AI team operates the platform live for all 3 festival days. Your team never has to manage the technology. That is our responsibility.
🌍
Ready for a Traveling Audience
Attendees arrive from NYC, London, Toronto, and Atlanta, most with no knowledge of DC. The platform guides every one of them from the moment they arrive.
📈
A Compounding Asset
This is the second festival, built to the standard of one with 15 years behind it. The data captured this year makes Year 3 sharper, and gives leadership hard numbers to grow on.
The Outcome
The Festival, With and
Without This Platform.
Without It
  • Attendees walk past vendors without stopping
  • Vendors guess what sells and when to restock

  • No data after the weekend. Year 3 starts cold.
→
With It
  • Attendees browse and buy from anywhere on-site
  • Vendors restock on live data and AI coaching

  • Full data captured. Year 3 starts ahead.

Vendors generate more revenue. Higher earnings drive vendor retention and word-of-mouth referrals.
💛
Attendees get a stronger experience. The experience is what converts a one-time ticket into an annual one.

Leadership operates with control. Real-time visibility means fewer surprises and faster decisions all weekend.

The festival owns its proof. Hard numbers to take to sponsors and to justify booth pricing in Year 3.

The Cost Comparison
What the Market
Charges for This.
Be clear on the scope of what is on the table. This is not a single app. It is four connected platforms, a live festival operation, and a dedicated team. At standard industry rates, here is what each component costs sourced separately.
A custom-built festival app from a development agency
$25,000+
A separate vendor management dashboard
$12,000+
An owner analytics and reporting system
$10,000+
A dedicated project manager for a 4-month build
$12,000+
An on-site technical support team for all 3 festival days
$8,000+
Ongoing hosting and maintenance, billed monthly by an agency
$1,500/mo
What these pieces cost separately
$67,000+
Off-the-shelf event platforms start at $3,000 per event and lock you into their template, their branding, and their ceiling. This is a fully custom platform, built, owned, and operated end to end for Afro+ Fest.
Your investment starts at $15,750
The Investment
Two Paths
to a Yes.
Not a subscription. Not a template. A fully custom platform, built from the ground up and operated by a dedicated team across the entire festival weekend. Two ways to structure the investment.
Option A
Direct Partnership
with Elevate AI
  • Tier 1. Discovery & full platform build $6,500
  • Tier 2. Vendor onboarding & management $2,500
  • Tier 3. Festival weekend live ops, 3 days $4,750
  • Tier 4. Post-festival analytics report $2,000
Total Investment
$15,750
Option B
The "AI Advantage"
Vendor Add-On Model
  • Mike adds an "AI Advantage" fee to vendor registration You set it
  • Vendors pay for the AI Advantage as part of their package Per vendor
  • Anything collected above the Elevate AI fee Afro+ keeps it
  • Elevate AI receives the same base fee $15,750
Elevate AI Base Fee
$15,750
Either way, the platform is the same. Option B simply lets Afro+ turn the AI into a profit center instead of a cost.
Our Partnership Terms
Beyond the
Check.
This is not a build-and-walk-away contract. Elevate AI is proposing a partnership, as the official AI partner of Afro+ Fest. The terms below define that partnership. They protect both sides and position us to build this story together.
Partnership Terms
Six terms that define a genuine partnership, not a one-off contract
🏆 Official Partner Status. Elevate AI is named the official AI partner of Afro+ Fest, with Zuleema Isaac credited as Director of Festival AI Systems.
🌐 Brand Visibility. The Elevate AI logo appears on theafroplus.com, within vendor onboarding materials, and as a "Powered by Elevate AI" credit in the dashboard.
📊 Promotional Rights. Full rights to use this project as a case study, to reference the work publicly, to capture our team's behind-the-scenes content, and to a closing testimonial from Mike.
🔄 Renewal Priority. First right of refusal to renew the AI platform partnership for next year's festival.
✨ Add-On Revenue Rights. The right to offer the optional, opt-in in-app upgrades detailed below.
🎟️ On-Site Access. A designated work area to run the platform, complimentary VIP access for the team across all 3 days, and QR "Meet the AI Team" cards in approved areas.
✦ Optional In-App Revenue Add-Ons
Fully opt-in, never mandatory. Each one creates additional value for vendors and attendees, and an additional revenue stream for Elevate AI.
📊 Vendor Post-Festival Analytics Report. $65 per vendor, selected at onboarding. A complete breakdown of what sold, when it sold, and what to plan for next year.
✨ Attendee Premium Experience. $10 per weekend pass. Unlocks full Zuri personalization, a custom day-by-day schedule, and priority wishlist alerts.
🌟 Featured Vendor Spotlight. Priced per category. Premium in-app placement for vendors who want maximum visibility.
We are hoping to make this a flagship partnership, not a vendor transaction.
By building this right, Afro+ Fest becomes the first AI-backed festival.
